title: Photoacoustic-Ultrasound Dual-mode Imaging (PAUS)
sub_domain: Concurrent functional + anatomical imaging via dual-contrast acquisition (multi-physics joint inverse)
source_data_quality_note: v3 standalone multi-physics genesis Principle. Joint forward operator combines (i) quantitative photoacoustic tomography (qPAT — optical absorption + thermoelastic + acoustic chain, recovering chromophore concentrations) with (ii) pulse-echo ultrasound (acoustic Born / impedance contrast, recovering tissue echogenicity / impedance map). Same probe, same scan, dual contrast: PA for functional/molecular, US for anatomical/morphological. Joint inversion shares tissue speed-of-sound c(r) and density rho(r) between modalities, improving identifiability beyond either mode alone. Distinct from L1-041 PAT (acoustic-only photoacoustic), L1-504 qPAT (optical-thermoelastic-acoustic, no US), and L1-032 ultrasound (US-only) by the dual-contrast joint inversion. Authored under the standalone-as-default v3 design (see pwm-team/pwm_product/genesis/PWM_V3_STANDALONE_VS_COMPOSITE.md).

E.description: Photoacoustic-Ultrasound Dual-mode Imaging (PAUS): joint multi-physics forward couples (i) qPAT chain — optical fluence Phi(r, lambda) by radiative transfer, energy absorption H = mu_a * Phi, thermoelastic source p_0 = Gamma * H, acoustic propagation p_PA(r_d, t) = integral G_acoustic(r_d, r'; c) * p_0(r') dr'; (ii) pulse-echo ultrasound — incident wave from US transmit produces backscatter from tissue impedance contrast Z(r) = rho(r) * c(r), with received signal y_US(r_d, t) = integral G_pulse_echo(r_d, r'; c, rho) * (delta-Z/Z)(r') dr' under the Born approximation; (iii) shared tissue model — both modalities depend on the same tissue speed-of-sound c(r) and density rho(r) maps, with PA additionally constrained by mu_s'(r) and qPAT-specific parameters. The forward DAG has 9 primitives with two coupling constraints (n_c = 2): (i) shared tissue speed-of-sound and density across both modalities (parameter coupling); (ii) spatially co-registered acquisition (geometric coupling — same transducer, same scan plane). Recovery is posed as the joint inverse problem that recovers (mu_a(r, lambda), Z(r), c(r), rho(r)) from joint measurement {p_PA(r_d, t, lambda), y_US(r_d, t)}. Difficulty tier delta = 5 with raw condition number kappa ~ 280 (limited by PA/US bandwidth mismatch and absorbing-region ambiguity) and effective kappa_eff ~ 40 after model-based joint reconstruction. Mismatch parameters: pa_us_synchronization_jitter, fluence_inhomogeneity, acoustic_speed_heterogeneity, acoustic_attenuation, transducer_bandwidth_difference_pa_vs_us, motion_during_acquisition. Additive Gaussian thermal noise sets the data-fidelity floor. See forward_model field for the closed-form joint imaging equation.
E.forward_model: PA chain: Phi = G_optical * S; H = mu_a * Phi; p_0 = Gamma * H; p_PA(r_d, t, lambda) = integral G_acoustic(r_d, r'; c) * p_0(r', lambda) dr' + n_PA. US chain: y_US(r_d, t) = integral G_pulse_echo(r_d, r'; c, rho) * (delta-Z/Z)(r') dr' + n_US. Joint inversion: recover (mu_a(r, lambda), Z(r), c(r), rho(r)) from joint {p_PA, y_US} given mu_s', Gamma, optical illumination S, US transmit beam.
E.world_state_x: Joint state: (a) qPAT-class — 3D multi-spectral absorption coefficient mu_a(r, lambda) and chromophore concentrations (HbO2, Hb, water, lipid, melanin, contrast agents); (b) US-class — 3D acoustic impedance map Z(r) = rho(r) * c(r), tissue speed-of-sound c(r), density rho(r). Joint solution_space is 4D-spectral-functional-and-acoustic-anatomical.
E.observation_y: Joint dual-contrast measurement: (a) multi-wavelength PA pressure waveforms p_PA(r_d, t, lambda) at N_wavelengths typically 5-16 in 700-1064 nm range; (b) US pulse-echo waveforms y_US(r_d, t) at N_US_modes typically 1-5 (B-mode + harmonic + Doppler + shear-wave). Both acquired through the same transducer array at co-registered spatial-temporal grid.

G.dag: L.optical_source -> L.optical_diffusion -> L.beer_lambert -> L.thermoelastic_grueneisen -> L.acoustic_wave_propagation -> L.us_transmit_beam -> L.us_pulse_echo -> L.transducer_detection_dual -> int.spectral_temporal_spatial
coupling_constraints: shared_tissue_acoustic: both PA and US chains share tissue speed-of-sound c(r) and density rho(r); jointly co-estimating these reduces variance in both reconstructions; this is the principal multi-physics coupling that justifies joint inversion over independent PA + US

co_registered_acquisition: same physical transducer array detects both PA and US signals at spatially co-registered positions and synchronized time; geometric coupling allows direct joint reconstruction without registration error

W.regime: Existence of recovered joint state (mu_a, Z, c, rho) is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds under multi-illumination + multi-wavelength PA acquisition combined with US transmit-receive at adequate SNR; single-wavelength single-illumination PAUS suffers the same multiplicative non-uniqueness as single-wavelength qPAT (excluded from the spec range). Stability is moderately conditioned (kappa_eff ~ 40 after joint model-based reconstruction) — pa_us_synchronization_jitter dominates registration error; fluence_inhomogeneity dominates qPAT-class chromophore bias; acoustic_attenuation dominates US-class deep-tissue contrast; transducer_bandwidth_difference_pa_vs_us contributes a frequency-dependent reconstruction artifact (PA typically lower-frequency band 0.5-10 MHz, US higher 5-20 MHz). Joint Hadamard well-posedness for the coupled qPAT + US Born forward is established by the qPAT references (Bal-Uhlmann 2010, Bal-Ren 2011) plus the dual-mode literature: Niederhauser et al. 2005 (combined OAT/US), Beard 2011 (review), Wang LV 2008 (multiscale photoacoustic), Mehrmohammadi et al. 2013 (PA-US clinical), Park et al. 2017 (PAUS guided interventions).

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [joint qPAT + US ML reconstruction; alternating PA/US blocks with shared tissue model; FEM-based unified forward] | model-based iterative [k-Wave + Field II coupled solver] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [PAUS-Net dual-encoder]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||(mu_a, Z, c, rho)_hat - (mu_a, Z, c, rho)||_2 <= C1 * sqrt(sigma_PA^2 + sigma_US^2) / sqrt(N_PA_wavelengths * N_US_modes * N_transducers * N_time_samples). Coupled-forward bias adds + C2 * |pa_us_synchronization_jitter| + C3 * |acoustic_attenuation| + C4 * |transducer_bandwidth_difference| + C5 * ||state - state_s||_1 (best s-sparse approximation residual). Joint reconstruction achieves q=2 (O(1/k^2)) under shared-tissue regularization; alternating PA/US blocks achieve q=1 with model-bias floor. Deep-prior dual-encoder variants achieve faster initial convergence with bounded systematic bias.
C.complexity: O(H * W * Z * (N_PA_wavelengths + N_US_modes) * (transducer_count * time_samples)^(2/3)) per iteration via k-Wave + Field II coupled time-domain forward / adjoint; learned dual-encoder variants O(H W Z * F_theta_cost) per forward pass

joint_well_posedness_references: Niederhauser JJ, Jaeger M, Lemor R, Weber P, Frenz M (2005) Combined ultrasound and optoacoustic system for real-time high-contrast vascular imaging in vivo. IEEE Trans Med Imaging 24(4):436-440.
Wang LV (2008) Tutorial on photoacoustic microscopy and computed tomography. IEEE J Sel Top Quantum Electron 14(1):171-179.
Beard P (2011) Biomedical photoacoustic imaging. Interface Focus 1(4):602-631.
Mehrmohammadi M, Yoon SJ, Yeager D, Emelianov SY (2013) Photoacoustic imaging for cancer detection and staging. Curr Mol Imaging 2(1):89-105.
Park S, Karpiouk AB, Aglyamov SR, Emelianov SY (2017) Photoacoustic imaging-guided biopsy: a clinical system. SPIE Photons Plus Ultrasound 10064:100640F.
Cox BT, Arridge SR, Beard PC (2009) Quantitative spectroscopic photoacoustic imaging: A review. J Biomed Opt 17(6):061202.
Bal G, Uhlmann G (2010) Inverse diffusion theory of photoacoustics. Inverse Problems 26(8):085010.
Hauptmann A, Cox BT (2020) Deep learning in photoacoustic tomography: current approaches and future directions. J Biomed Opt 25(11):112903.

distinctness_audit.closest_existing_principle: L1-504 qPAT (just authored) and L1-032 US (tie)
distinctness_audit.rationale: L1-504 qPAT recovers mu_a(r, lambda) from PA only; n_c = 2 (fluence-to-absorption + thermoelastic-to-acoustic); solution_space = 3D_spectral_chromophore_quantitative. L1-032 US recovers tissue impedance / echogenicity from pulse-echo only; n_c = 0; solution_space = 2D/3D acoustic. L1-509 PAUS jointly inverts both modalities with shared tissue speed-of-sound and density; n_c = 2 (shared tissue acoustic + co-registered acquisition); solution_space = 4D_dual_contrast_functional_anatomical (recovers chromophore concentrations AND impedance map AND tissue acoustic properties). Adds L.us_transmit_beam, L.us_pulse_echo, L.transducer_detection_dual primitives. Jaccard distance over (sensing_mechanism, solution_space, primitives) ~ 0.40 from each parent — Distinct under v1 advisory thresholds (>= 0.30). Note: this Principle directly cites L1-504 qPAT in the related_principles field — the qPAT chain is reused as a sub-component but not as a multi-parent royalty link (per standalone-as-default design).

v3_metadata.clinical_context: Active commercial and clinical research: real-time dual-contrast imaging in oncology (breast cancer staging — combining PA hemoglobin sO2 with US lesion morphology), vascular imaging (carotid plaque), thyroid imaging, lymph-node mapping, photoacoustic-guided biopsy, neonatal brain imaging, preclinical small-animal. Commercial platforms: iThera Medical MSOT inSight/Acuity, Verasonics Vantage with PA add-on, FUJIFILM-VisualSonics Vevo LAZR, Endra Nexus 128. Imaging depth 1-5 cm for PA, 5-20 cm for US — joint mode covers superficial to mid-depth pathology with both functional (PA) and anatomical (US) information.
